prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock volatility patterns for large-cap industrial stocks like ITW, I applied a geometric Brownian motion model with assumed annual volatility of ~20%. For a 22-day period, this translates to approximately 4.2% standard deviation. The point estimate assumes slight positive drift typical of established industrial companies. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions around the log-returns, with widening bands to reflect increasing uncertainty at higher confidence levels.
